Class ArchiveDataLocation

java.lang.Object
recoder.io.ArchiveDataLocation
All Implemented Interfaces:
DataLocation
public class ArchiveDataLocation extends Object implements DataLocation
This class implements a data location, that describes an entry of a Java archive file. Currently supported formats are ZIP and JAR.

  • Field Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Field

    boolean

    returns false since archives do not even provide writa access.

    tells the location, that the earlier created input stream has been closed

    returns false since archives do not provide writa access.

  • Field Details

    • LOCATION_TYPE_ARCHIVE

      public static final String LOCATION_TYPE_ARCHIVE
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • ArchiveDataLocation

      public ArchiveDataLocation(ZipFile archive, String itemname)
      creates a new location object.
      Parameters:
      archive - the file that contains the archive
      itemname - the name of the item within the archive

    • ArchiveDataLocation

      public ArchiveDataLocation(String archivename, String itemname) throws IOException
      creates a new location object.
      Parameters:
      archivename - the name of the archive file
      itemname - the name of the item within the archive
      Throws:
      IOException

  • Method Details

    • getType

      public String getType()
      returns the "type" of the data location.
      Specified by:
      getType in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      LOCATION_TYPE_ARCHIVE

    • getFile

      public ZipFile getFile()
      returns the zip file of this archive.
      Returns:
      the zip file of this archive.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      returns a URL-like string representation of the location in the form <type>: <location-specific-name>, i.e. 
       file:/bin/sh url:http://mywww/myfile archive:recoder.zip:recoder/java/JavaProgramFactory.class
      Specified by:
      toString in interface DataLocation
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • hasReaderSupport

      public boolean hasReaderSupport()
      determines whether the data source provides a reader interface. If this is not the case, the reader is placed on top of an input stream, which causes efficiency losses.
      Specified by:
      hasReaderSupport in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      true iff the data location provides reader functionality

    • getInputStream

      public InputStream getInputStream() throws IOException
      returns an input stream that can be used to read the archive entry content.
      Specified by:
      getInputStream in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      the input stream for reading the data
      Throws:
      IOException - if the stream cannot be created

    • inputStreamClosed

      public void inputStreamClosed()
      tells the location, that the earlier created input stream has been closed
      Specified by:
      inputStreamClosed in interface DataLocation

    • getReader

      public Reader getReader() throws IOException
      returns a reader for the according data content. The reader is placed on top of an input stream.
      Specified by:
      getReader in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      the according reader
      Throws:
      IOException - thrown if an error occurs with retrieving the reader or the underlying input stream from the according data object.

    • readerClosed

      public void readerClosed()
      tells the location, that the earlier created reader has been closed
      Specified by:
      readerClosed in interface DataLocation

    • isWritable

      public boolean isWritable()
      returns false since archives do not provide writa access.
      Specified by:
      isWritable in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      false

    • hasWriterSupport

      public boolean hasWriterSupport()
      returns false since archives do not even provide writa access.
      Specified by:
      hasWriterSupport in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      false

    • getOutputStream

      public OutputStream getOutputStream()
      returns null
      Specified by:
      getOutputStream in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      null

    • outputStreamClosed

      public void outputStreamClosed()
      tells the location, that the earlier created output stream has been closed
      Specified by:
      outputStreamClosed in interface DataLocation

    • getWriter

      returns null
      Specified by:
      getWriter in interface DataLocation
      Returns:
      null

    • writerClosed

      tells the location, that the earlier created writer has been closed
      Specified by:
